Kikuyu: “Ndamwĩra maũndũ macio mothe nĩgeetha mũtikanae gũtiganĩria wĩtĩkio.
English: “I have said these things to you so that you wouldn’t be caused to stumble.

Kikuyu: Nĩmakamũingata thunagogi-inĩ; na ũhoro wa ma nĩ atĩ, kũrĩ hĩndĩ ĩgũũka rĩrĩa mũndũ o wothe ũkaamũũraga ageciiragia atĩ nĩ Ngai aratungatĩra.
English: They will put you out of the synagogues. Yes, the time is coming that whoever kills you will think that he offers service to God.

Kikuyu: Mageekaga maũndũ macio nĩ ũndũ matimenyete Baba o na kana makaamenya.
English: They will do these things† because they have not known the Father nor me.

Kikuyu: Ndamwĩra maũndũ macio nĩgeetha hĩndĩ ĩyo yakinya mũkaaririkana atĩ nĩndamwĩrĩte. Ndiamwĩrire maũndũ macio kĩambĩrĩria-inĩ tondũ ndaarĩ hamwe na inyuĩ.
English: But I have told you these things so that when the time comes, you may remember that I told you about them. I didn’t tell you these things from the beginning, because I was with you.

Kikuyu: “Rĩu nĩngũthiĩ kũrĩ ũrĩa wandũmire. No gũtirĩ o na ũmwe wanyu ũranjũũria atĩrĩ, ‘Ũrathiĩ kũ?’
English: But now I am going to him who sent me, and none of you asks me, ‘Where are you going?’

Kikuyu: Mwaiyũrwo nĩ kĩeha nĩ tondũ nĩndamwĩra maũndũ macio.
English: But because I have told you these things, sorrow has filled your heart.

Kikuyu: No ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, nĩ kwagĩrĩire niĩ thiĩ nĩ ũndũ wanyu. Tondũ ndaaga gũthiĩ, Mũteithia ndagooka kũrĩ inyuĩ. No ndaathiĩ, nĩngamũtũma oke kũrĩ inyuĩ.
English: Nevertheless I tell you the truth: It is to your advantage that I go away; for if I don’t go away, the Counselor won’t come to you. But if I go, I will send him to you.

Kikuyu: Nake oka, nĩakaiguithia andũ a gũkũ thĩ ũrĩa mahĩtĩtie ũhoro-inĩ wa mehia, na ũhoro-inĩ wa ũthingu, o na ũhoro-inĩ wa ciira:
English: When he has come, he will convict the world about sin, about righteousness, and about judgment;

Kikuyu: Nĩmahĩtĩtie ũhoro-inĩ wa mehia, nĩgũkorwo andũ matinjĩtĩkĩtie;
English: about sin, because they don’t believe in me;

Kikuyu: nĩmahĩtĩtie ũhoro-inĩ wa ũthingu, nĩgũkorwo ndĩrathiĩ kũrĩ Baba kũrĩa mũtangĩcooka kũnyona;
English: about righteousness, because I am going to my Father, and you won’t see me any more;

Kikuyu: na nĩmahĩtĩtie ũhoro-inĩ wa ciira tondũ mũnene wa gũkũ thĩ nĩarĩkĩtie gũtuĩrwo ciira.
English: about judgment, because the prince of this world has been judged.

Kikuyu: “Ndĩ na maũndũ mangĩ maingĩ ma kũmwĩra, makĩria ya marĩa mũngĩhota kũũmĩrĩria rĩu.
English: “I still have many things to tell you, but you can’t bear them now.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, Roho Mũtheru ũcio wa ma oka, nĩakamũtongoria ũhoro-inĩ ũrĩa wa ma wothe. Ndakaaragia ũhoro wake mwene; akaaragia ũhoro wa maũndũ marĩa aiguĩte, ningĩ nĩakamwĩra ũhoro wa maũndũ marĩa magooka thuutha.
English: However, when he, the Spirit of truth, has come, he will guide you into all truth, for he will not speak from himself; but whatever he hears, he will speak. He will declare to you things that are coming.

Kikuyu: Nĩakangoocithia nĩgũkorwo nĩakamũmenyithia ũhoro wakwa ũrĩa arutĩte kuuma kũrĩ niĩ.
English: He will glorify me, for he will take from what is mine and will declare it to you.

Kikuyu: Maũndũ mothe ma Baba nĩ makwa. Nĩkĩo ndamwĩra atĩ Roho nĩakamũmenyithia ũhoro wakwa ũrĩa arutĩte kuuma kũrĩ niĩ, mũũmenye.
English: All things that the Father has are mine; therefore I said that he takes‡ of mine and will declare it to you.

Kikuyu: “Thuutha wa kahinda kanini mũtikũnyona rĩngĩ na ningĩ thuutha wa kahinda kanini nĩmũkũny- ona.”
English: “A little while, and you will not see me. Again a little while, and you will see me.”

Kikuyu: Amwe a arutwo ake makĩũrania atĩrĩ, “Arenda kuuga atĩa rĩrĩa aroiga atĩ, ‘Thuutha wa kahinda kanini mũtikũnyona rĩngĩ na ningĩ atĩ thuutha wa kahinda kanini nĩmũkũnyona’, ningĩ atĩ ‘Tondũ ndĩrathiĩ kũrĩ Baba?’ ”
English: Some of his disciples therefore said to one another, “What is this that he says to us, ‘A little while, and you won’t see me, and again a little while, and you will see me;’ and, ‘Because I go to the Father’?”

Kikuyu: Magĩthiĩ na mbere kũũrania atĩrĩ, “Arenda kuuga atĩa rĩrĩa aroiga atĩ, ‘Kahinda kanini’? Ithuĩ tũtiramenya ũrĩa aroiga.”
English: They said therefore, “What is this that he says, ‘A little while’? We don’t know what he is saying.”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩona atĩ nĩmendaga kũmũũria ũhoro ũcio, nĩ ũndũ ũcio akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Nĩ kũũrania mũrorania ũrĩa ngwendaga kuuga rĩrĩa njugire atĩ ‘Thuutha wa kahinda kanini mũtikũnyona rĩngĩ, na ningĩ thuutha wa kahinda kanini nĩmũkũnyona’?
English: Therefore Jesus perceived that they wanted to ask him, and he said to them, “Do you inquire among yourselves concerning this, that I said, ‘A little while, and you won’t see me, and again a little while, and you will see me’?

Kikuyu: Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, mũkaarĩra na mũcakae rĩrĩa andũ a thĩ marakena. Inyuĩ mũkanyiitwo nĩ kĩeha, no kĩeha, kĩanyu nĩgĩkagarũrũka gĩtuĩke gĩkeno.
English: Most certainly I tell you that you will weep and lament, but the world will rejoice. You will be sorrowful, but your sorrow will be turned into joy.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ-wa-nja agĩciara nĩakoragwo na ruo tondũ hĩndĩ yake nĩ nginyu; no aarĩkia gũciara kaana, akariganĩrwo nĩ ruo nĩ ũndũ wa gũkena nĩgũkorwo kaana nĩ gaaciarwo gũkũ thĩ.
English: A woman, when she gives birth, has sorrow because her time has come. But when she has delivered the child, she doesn’t remember the anguish any more, for the joy that a human being is born into the world.

Kikuyu: O na inyuĩ noguo: Rĩu nĩ ihinda rĩanyu rĩa kĩeha, no nĩngamuona rĩngĩ na nĩmũgakena, na gũtirĩ mũndũ o na ũmwe ũkaamũtunya gĩkeno kĩu.
English: Therefore you now have sorrow, but I will see you again, and your heart will rejoice, and no one will take your joy away from you.

Kikuyu: Mũthenya ũcio wakinya mũtigacooka kũnjũũria ũndũ o na ũrĩkũ. Ngũmwĩraatĩrĩnama, Babanĩakamũhekĩrĩagĩothemũkaahooyathĩinĩwarĩĩtwarĩakwa.
English: “In that day you will ask me no questions. Most certainly I tell you, whatever you may ask of the Father in my name, he will give it to you.

Kikuyu: Nginyagia rĩu mũtirĩ mwahooya ũndũ thĩinĩ wa rĩĩtwa rĩakwa. Hooyai na nĩmũkũheo, nĩguo gĩkeno kĩanyu gĩkinyanĩre.
English: Until now, you have asked nothing in my name. Ask, and you will receive, that your joy may be made full.

Kikuyu: “O na gũtuĩka ngoretwo ngĩaria na ngerekano-rĩ, hĩndĩ nĩĩroka rĩrĩa itagacooka kũmwarĩria na njĩra ĩyo, no ngaamwĩraga ũhoro wa Baba itekũhithĩrĩra.
English: “I have spoken these things to you in figures of speech. But the time is coming when I will no more speak to you in figures of speech, but will tell you plainly about the Father.

Kikuyu: Mũthenya ũcio nĩmũkahooyaga thĩinĩ wa rĩĩtwa rĩakwa. Ndiroiga atĩ nĩngahooyaga Baba ithenya rĩanyu.
English: In that day you will ask in my name; and I don’t say to you that I will pray to the Father for you,

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, Baba we mwene nĩamwendete, nĩgũkorwo inyuĩ nĩmũnyendete, na nĩmwĩtĩkĩtie atĩ ndoimire kũrĩ Ngai.
English: for the Father himself loves you, because you have loved me, and have believed that I came from God.

Kikuyu: Niĩ ndoimire kũrĩ Baba, ngĩũka gũkũ thĩ; na rĩu nĩnguuma gũkũ thĩ, njooke kũrĩ Baba.”
English: I came from the Father and have come into the world. Again, I leave the world and go to the Father.”

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo arutwo a Jesũ makiuga atĩrĩ, “Rĩu nĩũratũhe ũhoro wega, ũtekwaria na ngerekano.
English: His disciples said to him, “Behold, now you are speaking plainly, and using no figures of speech.

Kikuyu: Rĩu nĩtwamenya atĩ wee nĩũũĩ maũndũ mothe, na atĩ o na ndũbataire mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ akũũrie ciũria. Ũhoro ũcio nĩwatũma twĩtĩkie atĩ woimĩte kũrĩ Ngai.”
English: Now we know that you know all things, and don’t need for anyone to question you. By this we believe that you came from God.”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Rĩu nĩmwetĩkia!
English: Jesus answered them, “Do you now believe?

Kikuyu: No hĩndĩ nĩĩroka, na nĩnginyu, rĩrĩa mũkahurunjũka, o mũndũ athiĩ gwake mũciĩ. Mũkaandiga ndĩ nyiki, no ndirĩ nyiki, nĩgũkorwo Baba arĩ hamwe na niĩ.
English: Behold, the time is coming, yes, and has now come, that you will be scattered, everyone to his own place, and you will leave me alone. Yet I am not alone, because the Father is with me.

Kikuyu: “Ndamwĩra maũndũ macio nĩgeetha mũgĩe na thayũ thĩinĩ wakwa. Gũkũ thĩ nĩmũrĩonaga thĩĩna. No ũmĩrĩriai! Niĩ nĩndooretie thĩ.”
English: I have told you these things, that in me you may have peace. In the world you have trouble; but cheer up! I have overcome the world.”

Kikuyu: Jesũ aarĩkia kuuga maũndũ macio, agĩtiira maitho akĩrora na igũrũ, akĩhooya, akiuga atĩrĩ: “Baba hĩndĩ nĩ nginyu. Goocithia Mũrũguo, nĩguo nake Mũrũguo akũgoocithie.
English: Jesus said these things, then lifting up his eyes to heaven, he said, “Father, the time has come. Glorify your Son, that your Son may also glorify you;

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo nĩũmũheete ũhoti wa gwatha andũ othe, nĩguo arĩa othe ũmũheete amahe muoyo wa tene na tene.
English: even as you gave him authority over all flesh, so he will give eternal life to all whom you have given him.

Kikuyu: Na rĩu ũyũ nĩguo muoyo wa tene na tene: Atĩ makũmenye, o Wee Ngai ũrĩa wa ma, na mamenye Jesũ Kristũ, ũrĩa ũtũmĩte.
English: This is eternal life, that they should know you, the only true God, and him whom you sent, Jesus Christ.

Kikuyu: Nĩngũgoocithĩtie gũkũ thĩ na ũndũ wa kũrĩĩkia wĩra ũrĩa waheire ndute.
English: I glorified you on the earth. I have accomplished the work which you have given me to do.

Kikuyu: Na rĩu Baba, ngoocithia ndĩ mbere yaku na riiri ũrĩa ndaarĩ naguo hamwe nawe o mbere thĩ ĩtaanagĩa.
English: Now, Father, glorify me with your own self with the glory which I had with you before the world existed.

Kikuyu: “Nĩnguũrĩirie andũ arĩa waheire gũkũ thĩ rĩĩtwa rĩaku. Acio maarĩ aku; nawe ũkĩĩhe o, nao nĩmathĩkĩire kiugo gĩaku.
English: “I revealed your name to the people whom you have given me out of the world. They were yours, and you have given them to me. They have kept your word.

Kikuyu: Na rĩu nĩmamenyete atĩ indo iria ciothe ũheete ciumĩte kũrĩ wee.
English: Now they have known that all things whatever you have given me are from you,

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo nĩndamaheire ciugo iria waheire, nao magĩciĩtĩkĩra. Nĩmamenyire hatarĩ nganja atĩ ndoimire kũrĩ wee, na magĩĩtĩkia atĩ wee nĩwe wandũmire.
English: for the words which you have given me I have given to them; and they received them, and knew for sure that I came from you. They have believed that you sent me.

Kikuyu: Acio nĩo ndĩrahoera. Ndirahoera andũ a gũkũ thĩ, no ndĩrahoera arĩa ũũheete, nĩgũkorwo nĩ aku.
English: I pray for them. I don’t pray for the world, but for those whom you have given me, for they are yours.

Kikuyu: Kĩrĩa gĩothe ndĩ nakĩo nĩ gĩaku, na kĩrĩa gĩothe ũrĩ nakĩo nĩ gĩakwa. Naguo ũgooci nĩũũkĩte kũrĩ niĩ nĩ ũndũ wao.
English: All things that are mine are yours, and yours are mine, and I am glorified in them.

Kikuyu: Ndigũikara gũkũ thĩ rĩngĩ, no-o marĩ o gũkũ thĩ, na niĩ ndĩroka kũrĩ wee. Baba Mũtheru, magitĩre na ũhoti wa rĩĩtwa rĩaku, rĩĩtwa rĩrĩa waheire, nĩgeetha matuĩke ũmwe o ta ũrĩa ithuĩ tũrĩ ũmwe.
English: I am no more in the world, but these are in the world, and I am coming to you. Holy Father, keep them through your name which you have given me, that they may be one, even as we are.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa ndĩraarĩ nao, nĩndĩramagitagĩra na ngamamenyerera na rĩĩtwa rĩu waheire. Gũtirĩ o na ũmwe ũũrĩte o tiga ũrĩa wathĩrĩirwo kwanangwo nĩguo Maandĩko mahingio.
English: While I was with them in the world, I kept them in your name. I have kept those whom you have given me. None of them is lost except the son of destruction, that the Scripture might be fulfilled.

Kikuyu: “Rĩu ndĩroka kũrĩ wee, no maũndũ maya ndĩroiga ndĩ o gũkũ thĩ, nĩguo maiyũrwo nĩ gĩkeno gĩakwa kĩrĩ gĩkinyanĩru kũna thĩinĩ wao.
English: But now I come to you, and I say these things in the world, that they may have my joy made full in themselves.

Kikuyu: Nĩndĩmaheete kiugo gĩaku, nao andũ a gũkũ thĩ makamathũũra, nĩgũkorwo o ti a gũkũ thĩ rĩngĩ, o ta ũrĩa niĩ itarĩ wa gũkũ thĩ.
English: I have given them your word. The world hated them because they are not of the world, even as I am not of the world.

Kikuyu: Ihooya rĩakwa ti ũmarute gũkũ thĩ, no nĩ atĩ ũmagitagĩre kuuma kũrĩ ũrĩa mũũru.
English: I pray not that you would take them from the world, but that you would keep them from the evil one.

Kikuyu: O ti a gũkũ thĩ, o ta ũrĩa o na niĩ itarĩ wakuo.
English: They are not of the world, even as I am not of the world.

Kikuyu: Maamũre, ũmatherie na ũhoro ũrĩa wa ma; ũhoro waku nĩguo ũhoro wa ma.
English: Sanctify them in your truth. Your word is truth.*

Kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa wandũmire gũkũ thĩ, na niĩ noguo ndamatũma kũrĩ andũ a gũkũ thĩ.
English: As you sent me into the world, even so I have sent them into the world.

Kikuyu: Ndĩĩtheretie nĩ ũndũ wao, nĩguo o nao matherio kũna nĩ ũhoro ũrĩa wa ma.
English: For their sakes I sanctify myself, that they themselves also may be sanctified in truth.

Kikuyu: “Ningĩ to aya oiki ndĩrahoera. Ndĩrahoera o na arĩa angĩ makaanjĩtĩkia nĩ ũndũ wa ndũmĩrĩri ya aya,
English: “Not for these only do I pray, but for those also who will believe in me through their word,

Kikuyu: nĩguo othe matuĩke ũmwe Baba, o ta ũrĩa we ũrĩ thĩinĩ wakwa na niĩ ndĩ thĩinĩ waku. O nao marogĩa thĩinĩ witũ nĩgeetha andũ a gũkũ thĩ metĩkie atĩ nĩwe ũndũmĩte.
English: that they may all be one; even as you, Father, are in me, and I in you, that they also may be one in us; that the world may believe that you sent me.

Kikuyu: Nĩndĩmaheete riiri ũrĩa waheire, nĩguo matuĩke ũmwe o ta ũrĩa ithuĩ tũrĩ ũmwe,
English: The glory which you have given me, I have given to them, that they may be one, even as we are one,

Kikuyu: niĩ ndĩ thĩinĩ wao, nawe ũrĩ thĩinĩ wakwa. Marotuĩka ũmwe kũna, nĩgeetha andũ a gũkũ thĩ mamenye atĩ nĩwe wandũmire, na atĩ nĩũmendete o ta ũrĩa ũnyendete.
English: I in them, and you in me, that they may be perfected into one, that the world may know that you sent me and loved them, even as you loved me.

Kikuyu: “Baba, ngwenda arĩa ũũheete makorwo na niĩ harĩa ndĩ na mone riiri wakwa, riiri ũrĩa ũũheete nĩ ũndũ nĩwanyendete thĩ ĩtanombwo.
English: Father, I desire that they also whom you have given me be with me where I am, that they may see my glory which you have given me, for you loved me before the foundation of the world.

Kikuyu: “Baba Mũthingu, o na gũtuĩka andũ a gũkũ thĩ matikũũĩ-rĩ, niĩ nĩngũũĩ, nao nĩ moĩ atĩ nĩwe ũndũmĩte.
English: Righteous Father, the world hasn’t known you, but I knew you; and these knew that you sent me.

Kikuyu: Nĩngũmenyithanĩtie kũrĩ o, na nĩngũkĩrĩrĩria gũkũmenyithania, nĩgeetha wendani ũrĩa ũnyendete naguo ũikarage thĩinĩ wao na nĩguo o na niĩ mwene ngoragwo thĩinĩ wao.”
English: I made known to them your name, and will make it known; that the love with which you loved me may be in them, and I in them.”

Kikuyu: Jesũ aarĩkia kũhooya, agĩthiĩ arĩ na arutwo ake, makĩringa mũkuru wa Karũũĩ ga Kidironi. Mũrĩmo ũcio ũngĩ nĩ haarĩ na mũgũnda wa mĩtamaiyũ, agĩtoonya ho na arutwo ake.
English: When Jesus had spoken these words, he went out with his disciples over the brook Kidron, where there was a garden, into which he and his disciples entered.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, Judasi, ũrĩa wamũkunyanĩire, nĩooĩ handũ hau, nĩ ũndũ Jesũ nĩacemanagĩria ho kaingĩ na arutwo ake.
English: Now Judas, who betrayed him, also knew the place, for Jesus often met there with his disciples.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Judasi agĩthiĩ hau mũgũnda-inĩ ũcio, atongoretie mbũtũ ya thigari na anene amwe kuuma kũrĩ athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene na Afarisai. Nao nĩmakuuĩte icinga, na matawa, na indo cia mbaara.
English: Judas then, having taken a detachment of soldiers and officers from the chief priests and the Pharisees, came there with lanterns, torches, and weapons.

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ nĩ ũndũ nĩamenyaga maũndũ marĩa mothe maakirie kũmũkora-rĩ, akiumĩra akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Nũũ mũracaria?”
English: Jesus therefore, knowing all the things that were happening to him, went out and said to them, “Who are you looking for?”

Kikuyu: Nao makĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Nĩ Jesũ wa Nazarethi.” Nake Jesũ akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Niĩ nĩ niĩ we.” (Nake Judasi ũcio wamũkunyanĩire aarũngiĩ hau hamwe nao.)
English: They answered him, “Jesus of Nazareth.” Jesus said to them, “I am he.” Judas also, who betrayed him, was standing with them.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Jesũ aameerire atĩrĩ, “Niĩ nĩ niĩ we,” magĩcooka na thuutha makĩgũa thĩ.
English: When therefore he said to them, “I am he,” they went backward and fell to the ground.

Kikuyu: O rĩngĩ Jesũ akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Nũũ mũracaria?” Nao makĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Nĩ Jesũ wa Nazarethi.”
English: Again therefore he asked them, “Who are you looking for?” They said, “Jesus of Nazareth.”

Kikuyu: Jesũ akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Ndamwĩra atĩ nĩ niĩ we. Akorwo nĩ niĩ mũracaria-rĩ, rekei andũ aya mathiĩ.”
English: Jesus answered, “I told you that I am he. If therefore you seek me, let these go their way,”

Kikuyu: Gwekĩkire ũguo nĩgeetha ũhoro ũrĩa aarĩtie ũhinge, rĩrĩa oigire atĩrĩ, “Andũ arĩa waheire gũtirĩ o na ũmwe wao ndeete.”
English: that the word might be fulfilled which he spoke, “Of those whom you have given me, I have lost none.”*

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Simoni Petero, tondũ aarĩ na rũhiũ rwa njora, akĩrũcomora na agĩtinia gũtũ kwa ũrĩo kwa ndungata ya mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũrĩa mũnene. (Ndungata ĩyo yetagwo Maliku.)
English: Simon Peter therefore, having a sword, drew it, struck the high priest’s servant, and cut off his right ear. The servant’s name was Malchus.

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ agĩatha Petero atĩrĩ, “Cookia rũhiũ rwaku rwa njora! Gĩkombe kĩrĩa Baba aaheete-rĩ, nĩngwaga gũkĩnyuĩra?”
English: Jesus therefore said to Peter, “Put the sword into its sheath. The cup which the Father has given me, shall I not surely drink it?”

Kikuyu: Nayo mbũtũ ĩyo ya thigari na mũnene wayo, hamwe na anene acio a Ayahudi makĩnyiita Jesũ. Makĩmuoha na
English: So the detachment, the commanding officer, and the officers of the Jews seized Jesus and bound him,

Kikuyu: makĩamba kũmũtwara kwa Anasi, ũrĩa warĩ mũthoniwe wa Kaiafa, ũrĩa warĩ mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũrĩa mũnene mwaka ũcio.
English: and led him to Annas first, for he was father-in-law to Caiaphas, who was high priest that year.

Kikuyu: Kaiafa nĩwe wataarĩte Ayahudi atĩ no gũkorwo arĩ wega mũndũ ũmwe akuĩre andũ othe.
English: Now it was Caiaphas who advised the Jews that it was expedient that one man should perish for the people.

Kikuyu: Simoni Petero marĩ na mũrutwo ũngĩ makĩrũmĩrĩra Jesũ. Na tondũ mũrutwo ũcio nĩoĩo nĩ mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũrĩa mũnene, agĩtwarana na Jesũ kũu nja ya mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũcio mũnene.
English: Simon Peter followed Jesus, as did another disciple. Now that disciple was known to the high priest, and entered in with Jesus into the court of the high priest;

Kikuyu: No Petero agĩtigwo etereire hau nja ya kĩhingo. Nake mũrutwo ũcio ũngĩ, woĩo nĩ mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũrĩa mũnene, agĩcooka, akĩaria na mũirĩtu ũrĩa warĩ kĩhingo-inĩ agĩtoonyia Petero.
English: but Peter was standing at the door outside. So the other disciple, who was known to the high priest, went out and spoke to her who kept the door, and brought in Peter.

Kikuyu: Nake mũirĩtu ũcio warĩ kĩhingo-inĩ akĩũria Petero atĩrĩ, “Kaĩ wee ũrĩ ũmwe wa arutwo a mũndũ ũyũ?” Nake agĩcookia agĩkiuga atĩrĩ, “Aca, ndirĩ ũmwe wao.”
English: Then the maid who kept the door said to Peter, “Are you also one of this man’s disciples?” He said, “I am not.”

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, ndungata na thigari ciarũgamĩte irigiicĩirie mwaki ũrĩa ciakĩtie ikĩwota, tondũ kwarĩ na heho. Petero o nake aarũgamĩte hamwe nao agĩota mwaki.
English: Now the servants and the officers were standing there, having made a fire of coals, for it was cold. They were warming themselves. Peter was with them, standing and warming himself.

Kikuyu: Ihinda-inĩ o rĩu, mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũrĩa mũnene oragia Jesũ ũhoro wa arutwo ake na ũhoro wa ũrutani wake.
English: The high priest therefore asked Jesus about his disciples and about his teaching.

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Niĩ njarĩirie andũ othe a thĩ itarĩ ũndũ ngũhitha, na ngarutana hĩndĩ ciothe thunagogi-inĩ o na hekarũ-inĩ, kũrĩa Ayahudi othe macemanagia. Ndirĩ ũndũ njaragia na hitho.
English: Jesus answered him, “I spoke openly to the world. I always taught in synagogues and in the temple, where the Jews always meet. I said nothing in secret.

Kikuyu: Ũranjũria ũguo nĩkĩ? Ũria arĩa mananjigua ngĩaria. Ti-itherũ nĩmoĩ ũrĩa ndanoiga.”
English: Why do you ask me? Ask those who have heard me what I said to them. Behold, they know the things which I said.”

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Jesũ oigire ũguo, ũmwe wa thigari iria ciarĩ hau akĩmũgũtha rũhĩ, akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Ũguo nĩguo ũgũcookeria mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũrĩa mũnene?”
English: When he had said this, one of the officers standing by slapped Jesus with his hand, saying, “Do you answer the high priest like that?”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Angĩkorwo nĩndoiga ũũru-rĩ, heana ũira wa ũũru ũcio. No angĩkorwo ndaaria ũhoro wa ma-rĩ, wangũthĩra kĩ?”
English: Jesus answered him, “If I have spoken evil, testify of the evil; but if well, why do you beat me?”

Kikuyu: Nake Anasi akĩmũneana atwarwo arĩ o muohe kũrĩ Kaiafa mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũrĩa mũnene.
English: Annas sent him bound to Caiaphas, the high priest.

Kikuyu: O hĩndĩ ĩyo Simoni Petero arũgamĩte agĩota mwaki, akĩũrio atĩrĩ, “Kaĩ wee ũrĩ ũmwe wa arutwo a mũndũ ũyũ?” Nake agĩkaana, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Aca, ndirĩ ũmwe wao!”
English: Now Simon Peter was standing and warming himself. They said therefore to him, “You aren’t also one of his disciples, are you?” He denied it and said, “I am not.”

Kikuyu: Ndungata ĩmwe ya mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũrĩa mũnene, na yarĩ ya mbarĩ ya mũndũ ũrĩa Petero aatinĩtie gũtũ, ĩkĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Na githĩ niĩ ndirakuonire mũrĩ nake hau mũgũnda-inĩ wa mĩtamaiyũ?”
English: One of the servants of the high priest, being a relative of him whose ear Peter had cut off, said, “Didn’t I see you in the garden with him?”

Kikuyu: O rĩngĩ Petero agĩkaana, na o hĩndĩ ĩyo ngũkũ ĩgĩkũga.
English: Peter therefore denied it again, and immediately the rooster crowed.

Kikuyu: Ningĩ Ayahudi makĩruta Jesũ gwa Kaiafa makĩmũtwara gĩikaro-inĩ kĩa Barũthi wa Kĩroma. Hĩndĩ ĩyo kwarĩ rũciinĩ tene, na nĩgeetha matikagwatwo nĩ thaahu, Ayahudi matiatoonyire gĩikaro kĩu; nĩ ũndũ nĩmendaga mahote kũrĩa gĩathĩ kĩa Bathaka.
English: They led Jesus therefore from Caiaphas into the Praetorium. It was early, and they themselves didn’t enter into the Praetorium, that they might not be defiled, but might eat the Passover.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Pilato akiuma akĩmakora kũu nja akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Mũndũ ũyũ mũramũthitangĩra kĩ?”
English: Pilate therefore went out to them and said, “What accusation do you bring against this man?”

Kikuyu: Nao makĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Atangĩrĩ muuni watho-rĩ, tũtingĩmũrehe kũrĩ we.”
English: They answered him, “If this man weren’t an evildoer, we wouldn’t have delivered him up to you.”

Kikuyu: Nake Pilato akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Kĩmuoei inyuĩ ene mũkamũciirithie kũringana na watho wanyu.” Nao Ayahudi makĩregana na ũhoro ũcio, makiuga atĩrĩ, “Ithuĩ tũtirĩ na rũũtha rwa kũũraga mũndũ o na ũ.”
English: Pilate therefore said to them, “Take him yourselves, and judge him according to your law.” Therefore the Jews said to him, “It is illegal for us to put anyone to death,”

Kikuyu: Maũndũ maya mekĩkire nĩgeetha ciugo iria Jesũ aarĩtie akĩonania mũthemba wa gĩkuũ kĩrĩa agaakua ihingio.
English: that the word of Jesus might be fulfilled, which he spoke, signifying by what kind of death he should die.

Kikuyu: Pilato agĩcooka agĩtoonya gĩikaro kĩu kĩa Barũthi, agĩĩta Jesũ akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Wee nĩwe mũthamaki wa Ayahudi?”
English: Pilate therefore entered again into the Praetorium, called Jesus, and said to him, “Are you the King of the Jews?”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Ũcio nĩ woni waku, kana nĩ andũ makwĩrĩte ũhoro wakwa?”
English: Jesus answered him, “Do you say this by yourself, or did others tell you about me?”

Kikuyu: Nake Pilato akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Niĩ ndĩkĩrĩ Mũyahudi? Andũ anyu na athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene nĩo makũneanĩte kũrĩ niĩ. Nĩ atĩa wĩkĩte?”
English: Pilate answered, “I’m not a Jew, am I? Your own nation and the chief priests delivered you to me. What have you done?”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Ũthamaki wakwa ti wa gũkũ thĩ. Korwo nĩ ũguo-rĩ, ndungata ciakwa nicingĩndũĩrĩire ndikanyiitwo nĩ Ayahudi. No ũthamaki wakwa nĩ wa kuuma kũndũ kũngĩ.”
English: Jesus answered, “My Kingdom is not of this world. If my Kingdom were of this world, then my servants would fight, that I wouldn’t be delivered to the Jews. But now my Kingdom is not from here.”

Kikuyu: Nake Pilato akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “nĩ ũndũ wa ũguo-rĩ, wee ũkĩrĩ Mũthamaki!” Nake Jesũ akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Nĩwaria ma kuuga atĩ niĩ ndĩ Mũthamaki. Ma nĩ atĩrĩ, gĩtũmi gĩkĩ nĩkĩo gĩatũmire njiarwo, na nĩkĩo ndokire gũkũ thĩ atĩ nyumbũrage ũhoro ũrĩa wa ma. Mũndũ o wothe ũrĩ mwena wa ũhoro wa ma nĩathikagĩrĩria.”
English: Pilate therefore said to him, “Are you a king then?” Jesus answered, “You say that I am a king. For this reason I have been born, and for this reason I have come into the world, that I should testify to the truth. Everyone who is of the truth listens to my voice.”

Kikuyu: Nake Pilato akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Ũhoro wa ma nĩ ũrĩkũ?” Aarĩkia kũũria ũguo akiuma nja o rĩngĩ agĩthiĩ kũrĩ Ayahudi akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ndirona gĩtũmi gĩa kũmũthitangĩra.
English: Pilate said to him, “What is truth?” When he had said this, he went out again to the Jews, and said to them, “I find no basis for a charge against him.

Kikuyu: No ningĩ nĩ mũtugo wanyu hĩndĩ ya Bathaka ndĩmuohorere mũndũ ũmwe. Nĩmũkwenda ndĩmuohorere ‘mũthamaki wa Ayahudi’?”
English: But you have a custom that I should release someone to you at the Passover. Therefore, do you want me to release to you the King of the Jews?”

Kikuyu: Nao makĩanĩrĩra, magĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Aca, tũtikwenda ũcio! Tuohorere Baraba!” Na rĩrĩ, Baraba ũcio aarĩ mũtunyani.
English: Then they all shouted again, saying, “Not this man, but Barabbas!” Now Barabbas was a robber.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Pilato akĩoya Jesũ, akĩmũhũũrithia iboko.
English: So Pilate then took Jesus and flogged him.

Kikuyu: Nacio thigari igĩtuma thũmbĩ ya mĩigua, ikĩmwĩkĩra mũtwe, na ikĩmũhumba kanjũ ya rangi wa ndathi,
English: The soldiers twisted thorns into a crown and put it on his head, and dressed him in a purple garment.

Kikuyu: igathiiaga harĩ we o ihinda kwa ihinda, ikamũnyũrũragia ikiugaga atĩrĩ, “Ngeithi, mũthamaki wa Ayahudi!” Nacio ikamũgũthaga ũthiũ na hĩ.
English: They kept saying, “Hail, King of the Jews!” and they kept slapping him.

Kikuyu: O rĩngĩ Pilato akiuma nja, akĩĩra Ayahudi atĩrĩ, “Ta onei ngũmũruta nja ndĩmũrehe kũrĩ inyuĩ, nĩguo mũmenye atĩ niĩ ndionete ũndũ o na ũrĩkũ angĩthitangĩrwo.”
English: Then Pilate went out again, and said to them, “Behold, I bring him out to you, that you may know that I find no basis for a charge against him.”

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Jesũ oimire nja ekĩrĩte thũmbĩ ĩyo ya mĩigua na ehumbĩte kanjũ ĩyo ya rangi wa ndathi-rĩ, Pilato akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Mũndũ nĩ ũyũ!”
English: Jesus therefore came out, wearing the crown of thorns and the purple garment. Pilate said to them, “Behold, the man!”

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene o na atongoria a Ayahudi maamuonire, makĩanĩrĩra makiuga atĩrĩ, “Mwambithie mũtĩ-igũrũ! Mwambithie mũtĩ-igũrũ!” No Pilato akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Muoei mũmwambe mũtĩ igũrũ arĩ inyuĩ. Ha ũhoro wakwa, ndirona ũndũ o na ũrĩkũ angĩthitangĩrwo.”
English: When therefore the chief priests and the officers saw him, they shouted, saying, “Crucify! Crucify!” Pilate said to them, “Take him yourselves and crucify him, for I find no basis for a charge against him.”

Kikuyu: Nao Ayahudi makiuga magwatĩirie atĩrĩ, “Ithuĩ nĩtũrĩ watho; na kũringana na watho ũcio no nginya akue, tondũ nĩetuĩte Mũrũ wa Ngai.”
English: The Jews answered him, “We have a law, and by our law he ought to die, because he made himself the Son of God.”

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Pilato aiguire ũguo, agĩĩtigĩra mũno makĩria,
English: When therefore Pilate heard this saying, he was more afraid.

Kikuyu: agĩcooka thĩinĩ wa nyũmba ĩyo ya ũthamaki. Ningĩ akĩũria Jesũ atĩrĩ, “Wee-rĩ, uumĩte kũ?” Nowe Jesũ ndaigana kũmũcookeria.
English: He entered into the Praetorium again, and said to Jesus, “Where are you from?” But Jesus gave him no answer.

Kikuyu: Pilato akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Kaĩ ũkũrega kũnjarĩria? Kaĩ ũtooĩ ndĩ na ũhoti wa gũkuohorithia, kana wa gũkwambithia mũtĩ igũrũ?”
English: Pilate therefore said to him, “Aren’t you speaking to me? Don’t you know that I have power to release you and have power to crucify you?”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Wee ndũngĩrĩ na ũhoti igũrũ rĩakwa korwo ndũheetwo guo kuuma igũrũ. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ũrĩa ũũneanire kũrĩwe nĩwe ũrĩ na mehia marĩa manene.”
English: Jesus answered, “You would have no power at all against me, unless it were given to you from above. Therefore he who delivered me to you has greater sin.”

Kikuyu: Kuuma hĩndĩ ĩyo, Pilato akĩgeria kuohora Jesũ, no Ayahudi magĩthiĩ na mbere kwanĩrĩra atĩrĩ, “Ũngĩrekereria mũndũ ũyũ, wee ndũrĩ mũrata wa Kaisari. Mũndũ o wothe wĩtuaga mũthamaki nĩamenete Kaisari.”
English: At this, Pilate was seeking to release him, but the Jews cried out, saying, “If you release this man, you aren’t Caesar’s friend! Everyone who makes himself a king speaks against Caesar!”

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Pilato aiguire ũguo-rĩ, akiumia Jesũ nja, na agĩikarĩra gĩtĩ kĩa mũtuanĩri ciira handũ hoĩkaine ta “Harĩa-haare-Mahiga” (na heetagwo na Kĩhibirania Gabatha).
English: When Pilate therefore heard these words, he brought Jesus out and sat down on the judgment seat at a place called “The Pavement”, but in Hebrew, “Gabbatha.”

Kikuyu: Kwarĩ mũthenya wa Ihaarĩria rĩa Kiumia gĩa Gĩathĩ kĩa Bathaka; na kwarĩ ta thaa thita. Nake Pilato akĩĩra Ayahudi atĩrĩ, “Ũyũ mũthamaki wanyu.”
English: Now it was the Preparation Day of the Passover, at about the sixth hour.† He said to the Jews, “Behold, your King!”

Kikuyu: No-o makĩanĩrĩra, makiuga atĩrĩ, “Mweherie! Mweherie! Mwambithie mũtĩ-igũrũ!” Nake Pilato akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Nyambithie Mũthamaki wanyu?” Nao athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene magĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Ithuĩ tũtirĩ mũthamaki ũngĩ tiga Kaisari.”
English: They cried out, “Away with him! Away with him! Crucify him!” Pilate said to them, “Shall I crucify your King?” The chief priests answered, “We have no king but Caesar!”

Kikuyu: Mũthia-inĩ Pilato akĩmũneana kũrĩ o nĩguo akambwo mũtĩ-igũrũ. Nacio thigari ikĩnyiita Jesũ.
English: So then he delivered him to them to be crucified. So they took Jesus and led him away.

Kikuyu: Na ekuuĩire mũtharaba wake agĩthiĩ nginya harĩa heetagwo “Ha Ihĩndĩ rĩa Mũtwe” (na no ho hetagwo na Kĩhibirania, Gologotha.)
English: He went out, bearing his cross, to the place called “The Place of a Skull”, which is called in Hebrew, “Golgotha”,

Kikuyu: Hau nĩho maamwambire, na hamwe nake makĩamba andũ angĩ eerĩ, ũmwe mwena ũmwe na ũcio ũngĩ mwena ũcio ũngĩ; nake Jesũ arĩ gatagatĩ.
English: where they crucified him, and with him two others, on either side one, and Jesus in the middle.

Kikuyu: Nake Pilato akĩandĩkithia kĩbaũ na gĩkĩhũũrĩrwo mũtharaba-inĩ. Nakĩo kĩandĩkĩtwo atĩrĩ, JESŨ WA NAZARETHI, MŨTHAMAKI WA AYAHUDI.
English: Pilate wrote a title also, and put it on the cross. There was written, “JESUS OF NAZARETH, THE KING OF THE JEWS.”

Kikuyu: Nao Ayahudi aingĩ magĩthoma maandĩko macio, nĩgũkorwo handũ harĩa Jesũ aambĩirwo haarĩ hakuhĩ na itũũra rĩu inene, nakĩo kĩbaũ kĩu kĩandĩkĩtwo na Kĩhibirania, na Kĩlatini, na Kĩyunani.*
English: Therefore many of the Jews read this title, for the place where Jesus was crucified was near the city; and it was written in Hebrew, in Latin, and in Greek.

Kikuyu: Nao athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene a Ayahudi makĩĩra Pilato atĩrĩ, “Tiga kwandĩka ‘Mũthamaki wa Ayahudi’, no andĩka atĩ mũndũ ũyũ nĩ kuuga oigire atĩ nĩwe mũthamaki wa Ayahudi.”
English: The chief priests of the Jews therefore said to Pilate, “Don’t write, ‘The King of the Jews,’ but, ‘he said, “I am King of the Jews.” ’ ”

Kikuyu: Nake Pilato akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Ũguo ndandĩka nĩndandĩka.”
English: Pilate answered, “What I have written, I have written.”

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa thigari ciarĩkirie kwamba Jesũ, ikĩoya nguo ciake, igĩcigayania maita mana, o mũndũ yake, no kanjũ yatigarire. Nguo ĩyo ndĩarĩ na rũtumo, yatumĩtwo kuuma igũrũ nginya thĩ ĩrĩ o taama ũmwe.
English: Then the soldiers, when they had crucified Jesus, took his garments and made four parts, to every soldier a part; and also the tunic. Now the tunic was without seam, woven from the top throughout.

Kikuyu: Nacio ikĩĩrana atĩrĩ, “Nĩtũtigei kũmĩtarũra; rekei tũmĩcuukĩre mĩtĩ nĩguo tũmenye ũrĩa ũgũthiĩ nayo.” Gwekĩkire ũguo nĩguo Maandĩko mahinge marĩa moigĩte atĩrĩ, “Maagayanire nguo ciakwa, na magĩcuukĩra kanjũ yakwa mĩtĩ.” Ũguo nĩguo thigari ciekire.
English: Then they said to one another, “Let’s not tear it, but cast lots for it to decide whose it will be,” that the Scripture might be fulfilled, which says, “They parted my garments among them. They cast lots for my clothing.”* Therefore the soldiers did these things.

Kikuyu: Hakuhĩ na mũtharaba wa Jesũ nĩho nyina aarũgamĩte, na mwarĩ wa nyina na nyina, na Mariamu mũtumia wa Kiliopa, na Mariamu Mũmagidali.
English: But standing by Jesus’ cross were his mother, his mother’s sister, Mary the wife of Clopas, and Mary Magdalene.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Jesũ onire nyina na mũrutwo ũrĩa endete marũngiĩ hakuhĩ nake, akĩĩra nyina atĩrĩ, “Mũtumia ũyũ, ũcio mũrũguo,”
English: Therefore when Jesus saw his mother, and the disciple whom he loved standing there, he said to his mother, “Woman, behold, your son!”

Kikuyu: agĩcooka akĩĩra mũrutwo ũcio atĩrĩ, “Ũcio maitũguo.” Kuuma hĩndĩ ĩyo mũrutwo ũcio akĩoya nyina wa Jesũ, agĩthiĩ gũtũũra mũciĩ kwa mũrutwo ũcio.
English: Then he said to the disciple, “Behold, your mother!” From that hour, the disciple took her to his own home.

Kikuyu: Thuutha ũcio, Jesũ amenya atĩ maũndũ mothe nĩmathira, na nĩguo Maandĩko mahinge, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Nĩnyootiĩ.”
English: After this, Jesus, seeing‡ that all things were now finished, that the Scripture might be fulfilled, said, “I am thirsty!”

Kikuyu: Hau nĩ haarĩ na cuba yarĩ na thiki, nĩ ũndũ ũcio thigari igĩtobokia thibũnji ho, igĩcooka igĩthecerera thibũnji ĩyo rũthanju-inĩ rwa mũthobi, ikĩmĩtwarĩrĩria kanua-inĩ ka Jesũ.
English: Now a vessel full of vinegar was set there; so they put a sponge full of the vinegar on hyssop, and held it at his mouth.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa Jesũ aamĩkundire, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Ũhoro nĩwathira.” Na oiga ũguo, akĩinamia mũtwe, agĩtuĩkana.
English: When Jesus therefore had received the vinegar, he said, “It is finished!” Then he bowed his head and gave up his spirit.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, kwarĩ mũthenya wa Ihaarĩria wa Ayahudi, nĩguo marokere Thabatũ ya mwanya. Na tondũ Ayahudi matiendaga mĩĩrĩ ĩyo ĩtigwo mĩtharaba-inĩ hĩndĩ ya Thabatũ, makĩũria Pilato aathane atĩ andũ acio maambĩtwo moinwo magũrũ, na mĩĩrĩ yao ĩcuurũrio.
English: Therefore the Jews, because it was the Preparation Day, so that the bodies wouldn’t remain on the cross on the Sabbath (for that Sabbath was a special one), asked of Pilate that their legs might be broken and that they might be taken away.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio thigari igĩthiĩ ikiuna magũrũ ma mũndũ wa mbere ũrĩa mambanĩirio na Jesũ na igĩcooka ikiuna ma ũcio ũngĩ.
English: Therefore the soldiers came and broke the legs of the first and of the other who was crucified with him;

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩa maakinyire harĩ Jesũ na makĩona atĩ nĩarĩkĩtie gũkua, makĩaga kuuna magũrũ make.
English: but when they came to Jesus and saw that he was already dead, they didn’t break his legs.

Kikuyu: Handũ ha ũguo-rĩ, mũthigari ũmwe agĩtheeca Jesũ mbaru na itimũ, na o rĩmwe hakiura thakame na maaĩ.
English: However, one of the soldiers pierced his side with a spear, and immediately blood and water came out.

Kikuyu: Nake mũndũ ũrĩa wonire maũndũ macio nĩarutĩte ũira, naguo ũira wake nĩ wa ma. We nĩooĩ atĩ aaragia ũhoro wa ma, na oimbũraga nĩgeetha o na inyuĩ mwĩtĩkie.
English: He who has seen has testified, and his testimony is true. He knows that he tells the truth, that you may believe.

Kikuyu: Namo maũndũ macio meekĩkire nĩgeetha Maandĩko mahinge marĩa moigĩte atĩrĩ, “Gũtirĩ ihĩndĩ o na rĩmwe rĩake rĩkoinwo.”
English: For these things happened that the Scripture might be fulfilled, “A bone of him will not be broken.”*

Kikuyu: O na ningĩ no harĩ Maandĩko mangĩ moigĩte atĩrĩ, “Makeerorera mũndũ ũrĩa maathecire.”
English: Again another Scripture says, “They will look on him whom they pierced.”*

Kikuyu: Thuutha ũcio, Jusufu wa Arimathea akĩhooya Pilato mwĩrĩ wa Jesũ. Na rĩrĩ, Jusufu aarĩ mũrutwo wa Jesũ, no amũrũmagĩrĩra na hitho tondũ wa gwĩtigĩra Ayahudi. Nake Pilato akĩmũhe rũtha, nake agĩthiĩ agĩkuua mwĩrĩ ũcio.
English: After these things, Joseph of Arimathaea, being a disciple of Jesus, but secretly for fear of the Jews, asked of Pilate that he might take away Jesus’ body. Pilate gave him permission. He came therefore and took away his body.

Kikuyu: Maathiire na Nikodemo, ũrĩa wathiĩte kũrĩ Jesũ ũtukũ. Nikodemo agĩtwara mũtukanio wa manemane na thubiri ingĩkinya o ta ratiri mĩrongo mũgwanja na ithano.
English: Nicodemus, who at first came to Jesus by night, also came bringing a mixture of myrrh and aloes, about a hundred Roman pounds.§

Kikuyu: Nao eerĩ makĩoya mwĩrĩ wa Jesũ, makĩwoha na mataama ma gatani hamwe na mĩtukanio ĩyo mĩnungi wega, kũringana na mĩtugo ya Ayahudi ya gũthikana.
English: So they took Jesus’ body, and bound it in linen cloths with the spices, as the custom of the Jews is to bury.

Kikuyu: Hau Jesũ aambĩirwo nĩ haarĩ na mũgũnda, na thĩinĩ wa mũgũnda ũcio nĩ kwarĩ na mbĩrĩra njerũ, ĩrĩa ĩtakoretwo ĩigĩtwo mwĩrĩ wa mũndũ o na ũmwe.
English: Now in the place where he was crucified there was a garden. In the garden was a new tomb in which no man had ever yet been laid.

Kikuyu: Tondũ kwarĩ mũthenya wa Ihaarĩria wa Ayahudi nĩguo marokere Thabatũ, na ningĩ nĩ ũndũ mbĩrĩra ĩyo yarĩ o hakuhĩ-rĩ, makĩiga Jesũ ho.
English: Then, because of the Jews’ Preparation Day (for the tomb was near at hand), they laid Jesus there.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, mũthenya wa mbere wa kwambĩrĩria kiumia rũciinĩ tene, kũrĩ o na nduma, Mariamu Mũmagidali nĩathiire mbĩrĩra-inĩ na akĩona ihiga rĩrĩa rĩarĩ mũromo-inĩ rĩrĩ rĩeherie.
English: Now on the first day of the week, Mary Magdalene went early, while it was still dark, to the tomb, and saw that the stone had been taken away from the tomb.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio agĩthiĩ atengʼerete kũrĩ Simoni Petero na mũrutwo ũrĩa ũngĩ, ũrĩa Jesũ endete, akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Nĩmarutĩte Mwathani mbĩrĩra-inĩ, na tũtiũĩ harĩa mamũigĩte!”
English: Therefore she ran and came to Simon Peter and to the other disciple whom Jesus loved, and said to them, “They have taken away the Lord out of the tomb, and we don’t know where they have laid him!”

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Petero na mũrutwo ũcio ũngĩ makiumagara marorete mbĩrĩra-inĩ.
English: Therefore Peter and the other disciple went out, and they went toward the tomb.

Kikuyu: Marĩ o eerĩ magĩtengʼera, no mũrutwo ũcio ũngĩ agĩkĩria Petero ihenya, agĩkinya mbĩrĩra-inĩ mbere.
English: They both ran together. The other disciple outran Peter and came to the tomb first.

Kikuyu: Akĩinamĩrĩra, na akĩrora thĩinĩ, akĩona mataama ma gatani marĩ maige thĩ, no ndaatoonyire.
English: Stooping and looking in, he saw the linen cloths lying there; yet he didn’t enter in.

Kikuyu: Nake Simoni Petero, ũrĩa warĩ thuutha wake, agĩkinya na agĩtoonya mbĩrĩra thĩinĩ. Akĩona mataama ma gatani maigĩtwo hau,
English: Then Simon Peter came, following him, and entered into the tomb. He saw the linen cloths lying,

Kikuyu: o hamwe na gĩtambaya kĩrĩa Jesũ ohetwo mũtwe agĩthikwo. Nakĩo gĩakũnjĩtwo gĩkaigwo kĩrĩ giiki, gĩtarĩ hamwe na mataama macio ma gatani.
English: and the cloth that had been on his head, not lying with the linen cloths, but rolled up in a place by itself.

Kikuyu: Thuutha ũcio mũrutwo ũcio ũngĩ, ũrĩa wakinyĩte mbĩrĩra-inĩ mbere, o nake agĩtoonya thĩinĩ. Akĩona na agĩĩtĩkia.
English: So then the other disciple who came first to the tomb also entered in, and he saw and believed.

Kikuyu: (Nĩgũkorwo nginyagia hĩndĩ ĩyo matiakoretwo mataũkĩirwo nĩ Maandĩko atĩ Jesũ nĩarĩ ariũke kuuma kũrĩ arĩa akuũ.)
English: For as yet they didn’t know the Scripture, that he must rise from the dead.

Kikuyu: Arutwo acio magĩcooka makĩinũka kwao,
English: So the disciples went away again to their own homes.

Kikuyu: no Mariamu akĩrũgama hau nja ya mbĩrĩra akĩrĩraga. O akĩrĩraga-rĩ, akĩinamĩrĩra, akĩrora mbĩrĩra thĩinĩ,
English: But Mary was standing outside at the tomb weeping. So as she wept, she stooped and looked into the tomb,

Kikuyu: akĩona araika eerĩ marĩ na nguo njerũ, maikarĩte thĩ harĩa mwĩrĩ wa Jesũ waigĩtwo, ũmwe agaikara mũtwe-inĩ nake ũcio ũngĩ agaikara magũrũ-inĩ.
English: and she saw two angels in white sitting, one at the head and one at the feet, where the body of Jesus had lain.

Kikuyu: Nao makĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Mũtumia ũyũ, nĩ kĩĩ kĩrakũrĩria?” Nake akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Nĩ ũndũ nĩmeheretie Mwathani wakwa mbĩrĩra-inĩ, na ndiũĩ harĩa mamũigĩte.”
English: They asked her, “Woman, why are you weeping?” She said to them, “Because they have taken away my Lord, and I don’t know where they have laid him.”

Kikuyu: O hĩndĩ ĩyo akĩĩhũgũra, akĩona Jesũ arũgamĩte hau, no ndaigana kũmenya atĩ aarĩ Jesũ.
English: When she had said this, she turned around and saw Jesus standing, and didn’t know that it was Jesus.

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Mũtumia ũyũ, nĩ kĩĩ kĩrakũrĩria? Nũũ ũracaria?” Mariamu agĩĩciiria atĩ ũcio aarĩ mũrĩmi wa mũgũnda, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Mũthuuri ũyũ, akorwo nĩwe ũmweheretie njĩĩra harĩa ũmũigĩte nĩguo thiĩ ngamuoe.”
English: Jesus said to her, “Woman, why are you weeping? Who are you looking for?” She, supposing him to be the gardener, said to him, “Sir, if you have carried him away, tell me where you have laid him, and I will take him away.”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmwĩta, “Mariamu!” Nake akĩĩhũgũra akĩmwĩtĩka na Kĩhibirania atĩrĩ, “Raboni!” (naguo nĩ kuuga Mũrutani).
English: Jesus said to her, “Mary.” She turned and said to him, “Rabboni!”† which is to say, “Teacher!”‡

Kikuyu: Jesũ akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ndũkae kũnyiita, nĩ ũndũ ndirĩ ndĩrambata thiĩ kũrĩ Baba! No rĩrĩ, thiĩ kũrĩ arutwo akwa, ũmeere atĩrĩ, ‘Nĩndĩracooka thiĩ kũrĩ Baba, na nowe Ithe wanyu, njooke kũrĩ Ngai wakwa, na nowe Ngai wanyu.’ ”
English: Jesus said to her, “Don’t hold me, for I haven’t yet ascended to my Father; but go to my brothers and tell them, ‘I am ascending to my Father and your Father, to my God and your God.’ ”

Kikuyu: Mariamu Mũmagidali agĩthiĩ kũrĩ arutwo, akĩmatwarĩra ũhoro, akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Nĩnyonete Mwathani!” Na akĩmeera atĩ nĩwe wamwĩrĩte maũndũ macio.
English: Mary Magdalene came and told the disciples that she had seen the Lord, and that he had said these things to her.

Kikuyu: Hwaĩ-inĩ wa mũthenya o ũcio wa mbere wa kwambĩrĩria kiumia, rĩrĩa arutwo maarĩ hamwe, na mĩrango ĩrĩ mĩhinge nĩ ũndũ wa gwĩtigĩra Ayahudi, Jesũ agĩũka akĩrũgama gatagatĩ-inĩ kao, akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Gĩai na thayũ!”
English: When therefore it was evening on that day, the first day of the week, and when the doors were locked where the disciples were assembled, for fear of the Jews, Jesus came and stood in the middle and said to them, “Peace be to you.”

Kikuyu: Aarĩkia kuuga ũguo akĩmoonia moko make na mbaru ciake. Nao arutwo makĩiyũrwo nĩ gĩkeno nĩ ũndũ wa kuona Mwathani.
English: When he had said this, he showed them his hands and his side. The disciples therefore were glad when they saw the Lord.

Kikuyu: Na o rĩngĩ Jesũ akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Gĩai na thayũ! O ta ũrĩa Baba aandũmire, noguo o na niĩ ndamũtũma.”
English: Jesus therefore said to them again, “Peace be to you. As the Father has sent me, even so I send you.”

Kikuyu: Na oiga ũguo, akĩmahuhĩrĩria mĩhũmũ yake, akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Amũkĩrai Roho Mũtheru.
English: When he had said this, he breathed on them, and said to them, “Receive the Holy Spirit!

Kikuyu: Arĩa othe mwarekera mehia mao, nĩmarĩrekagĩrwo, na arĩa othe mũtarĩrekagĩra, matirĩrekagĩrwo.”
English: If you forgive anyone’s sins, they have been forgiven them. If you retain anyone’s sins, they have been retained.”

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, Toma (ũrĩa wetagwo Wa-mahatha), ũmwe wa arĩa ikũmi na eerĩ, ndaarĩ hamwe na arutwo rĩrĩa Jesũ okire.
English: But Thomas, one of the twelve, called Didymus,§ wasn’t with them when Jesus came.

Kikuyu: Tondũ ũcio arutwo arĩa angĩ makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Nĩtuonete Mwathani.” Nowe akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Itoonete irema cia mĩcumarĩ moko-inĩ make, na ndoonyie kĩara gĩakwa hau irema-inĩ cia mĩcumarĩ, na njooke ndoonyie guoko mbaru-inĩ ciake, ndingĩĩtĩkia ũhoro ũcio.”
English: The other disciples therefore said to him, “We have seen the Lord!” But he said to them, “Unless I see in his hands the print of the nails, put my finger into the print of the nails, and put my hand into his side, I will not believe.”

Kikuyu: Thuutha wa kiumia kĩmwe, arutwo ake maarĩ kũu nyũmba rĩngĩ, na Toma aarĩ hamwe nao. O na gũtuĩka mĩrango yarĩ mĩhinge-rĩ, Jesũ agĩũka, akĩrũgama gatagatĩ-inĩ kao, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Gĩai na thayũ!”
English: After eight days, again his disciples were inside and Thomas was with them. Jesus came, the doors being locked, and stood in the middle, and said, “Peace be to you.”

Kikuyu: Agĩcooka akĩĩra Toma atĩrĩ, “Toonyia kĩara gĩaku haha; rora moko makwa. Tambũrũkia guoko gwaku ũgũikie mbaru-inĩ ciakwa. Tiga kũgĩa na nganja, ĩtĩkia.”
English: Then he said to Thomas, “Reach here your finger, and see my hands. Reach here your hand, and put it into my side. Don’t be unbelieving, but believing.”

Kikuyu: Nake Toma akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Mwathani wakwa na Ngai wakwa!”
English: Thomas answered him, “My Lord and my God!”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Wetĩkia tondũ nĩwanyona. Kũrathimwo nĩ arĩa metĩkagia matonete.”
English: Jesus said to him, “Because you have seen me,† you have believed. Blessed are those who have not seen and have believed.”

Kikuyu: Jesũ nĩaringire ciama ingĩ nyingĩ akĩonagwo nĩ arutwo ake, iria itaandĩkĩtwo Ibuku-inĩ rĩĩrĩ.
English: Therefore Jesus did many other signs in the presence of his disciples, which are not written in this book;

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, ici ciandĩkĩtwo nĩgeetha mwĩtĩkie atĩ Jesũ nĩwe Kristũ, Mũrũ wa Ngai, na atĩ na ũndũ wa gwĩtĩkia mũgĩe na muoyo thĩinĩ wa rĩĩtwa rĩake. 21
English: but these are written that you may believe that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God, and that believing you may have life in his name.

Kikuyu: Thuutha ũcio Jesũ nĩacookire akiumĩrĩra arutwo ake iria-inĩ rĩa Tiberia. Ũũ nĩguo kwahaanire:
English: After these things, Jesus revealed himself again to the disciples at the sea of Tiberias. He revealed himself this way.

Kikuyu: Simoni Petero, na Toma (ũrĩa wetagwo Wa-mahatha), na Nathanieli wa kuuma Kana ya Galili, na ariũ a Zebedi, o na arutwo angĩ eerĩ maarĩ hamwe.
English: Simon Peter, Thomas called Didymus,† Nathanael of Cana in Galilee, and the sons of Zebedee, and two others of his disciples were together.

Kikuyu: Simoni Petero akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Niĩ ndathiĩ gũtega thamaki.” Nao makĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Tũgũthiĩ nawe.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio makiumagara, magĩtoonya marikabu, no ũtukũ ũcio matirĩ kĩndũ maagwatirie.
English: Simon Peter said to them, “I’m going fishing.” They told him, “We are also coming with you.” They immediately went out and entered into the boat. That night, they caught nothing.

Kikuyu: Rũciinĩ tene, Jesũ akĩrũgama hũgũrũrũ-inĩ cia iria, no arutwo matiamenyaga atĩ nĩ Jesũ.
English: But when day had already come, Jesus stood on the beach; yet the disciples didn’t know that it was Jesus.

Kikuyu: Nake akĩmeeta akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Arata akwa, nĩmũgwatĩtie thamaki?” Nao magĩcookia makiuga atĩrĩ, “Aca.”
English: Jesus therefore said to them, “Children, have you anything to eat?” They answered him, “No.”

Kikuyu: Nake akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ikiai wabu mwena wa ũrĩo wa marikabu na nĩmũkũgwatia.” Rĩrĩa meekire ũguo, makĩremwo nĩ kũguucia wabu ũcio nĩ ũrĩa thamaki icio ciarĩ nyingĩ.
English: He said to them, “Cast the net on the right side of the boat, and you will find some.” They cast it therefore, and now they weren’t able to draw it in for the multitude of fish.

Kikuyu: Nake mũrutwo ũrĩa Jesũ eendete akĩĩra Petero atĩrĩ, “Nĩ Mwathani!” Na rĩrĩa Simoni Petero aiguire mũrutwo ũcio aamwĩra atĩ, “Nĩ Mwathani”, akĩĩoha nguo yake ya igũrũ (tondũ nĩamĩrutĩte), na akĩrũga maaĩ-inĩ.
English: That disciple therefore whom Jesus loved said to Peter, “It’s the Lord!” So when Simon Peter heard that it was the Lord, he wrapped his coat around himself (for he was naked), and threw himself into the sea.

Kikuyu: Nao arutwo acio angĩ makĩmũrũmĩrĩra na marikabu maguucĩtie wabu ũiyũrĩte thamaki tondũ matiarĩ haraaya, maarĩ ta itĩĩna rĩa makinya igana rĩmwe kuuma hũgũrũrũ-inĩ.
English: But the other disciples came in the little boat (for they were not far from the land, but about two hundred cubits‡ away), dragging the net full of fish.

Kikuyu: Maarĩkia kuumĩria marikabu, makĩona mwaki wa makara wakĩtio hau, na thamaki irĩ njĩkĩre riiko, na mũgate.
English: So when they got out on the land, they saw a fire of coals there, with fish and bread laid on it.

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Rehei thamaki imwe cia iria mwagwatia.”
English: Jesus said to them, “Bring some of the fish which you have just caught.”

Kikuyu: Nake Simoni Petero akĩhaica marikabu akĩguucia wabu ũcio nginya hũgũrũrũ-inĩ. Naguo waiyũrĩte thamaki iria nene, 153, no o na irĩ nyingĩ ũguo, wabu ũcio ndwatuĩkire.
English: Simon Peter went up, and drew the net to land, full of one hundred fifty-three great fish. Even though there were so many, the net wasn’t torn.

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ũkai mwĩgagũre.” Hatirĩ o na ũmwe wa arutwo acio wamũũririe atĩrĩ, “Nĩwe ũ?” Nĩmamenyire atĩ nĩ Mwathani.
English: Jesus said to them, “Come and eat breakfast!” None of the disciples dared inquire of him, “Who are you?” knowing that it was the Lord.

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ agĩthiĩ, akĩoya mũgate akĩmahe, agĩcooka akĩmahe thamaki o nacio.
English: Then Jesus came and took the bread, gave it to them, and the fish likewise.

Kikuyu: Rĩĩrĩ nĩrĩo rĩarĩ ihinda rĩa gatatũ rĩa Jesũ kuumĩrĩra arutwo ake thuutha wa kũriũka kuuma kũrĩ arĩa akuũ.
English: This is now the third time that Jesus was revealed to his disciples after he had risen from the dead.

Kikuyu: Nao maarĩkia kũrĩa, Jesũ akĩũria Simoni Petero atĩrĩ, “Simoni mũrũ wa Jona, na ma ti-itherũ nĩũnyendete gũkĩra aya?” Nake agĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Ĩĩ Mwathani, wee nĩũũĩ atĩ nĩngwendete.” Nake Jesũ akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Heaga tũgondu twakwa gĩa kũrĩa.”
English: So when they had eaten their breakfast, Jesus said to Simon Peter, “Simon, son of Jonah, do you love me more than these?” He said to him, “Yes, Lord; you know that I have affection for you.” He said to him, “Feed my lambs.”

Kikuyu: Ningĩ Jesũ akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Simoni mũrũ wa Jona, ti-itherũ nĩũnyendete?” Nake akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Ĩĩ Mwathani, wee nĩũũĩ atĩ nĩngwendete.” Nake Jesũ akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Rĩithagia ngʼondu ciakwa.”
English: He said to him again a second time, “Simon, son of Jonah, do you love me?” He said to him, “Yes, Lord; you know that I have affection for you.” He said to him, “Tend my sheep.”

Kikuyu: Agĩcooka akĩmũũria hĩndĩ ya gatatũ atĩrĩ, “Simoni mũrũ wa Jona, nĩũnyendete?” Nake Petero akĩigua ũũru ngoro nĩ ũndũ wa ũrĩa Jesũ aamũũririe hĩndĩ ya gatatũ atĩrĩ, “Nĩũnyen- dete?” Nake akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Mwathani, wee nĩũũĩ maũndũ mothe; wee nĩũũĩ atĩ nĩngwendete.” Nake Jesũ akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Heaga ngʼondu ciakwa gĩa kũrĩa.”
English: He said to him the third time, “Simon, son of Jonah, do you have affection for me?” Peter was grieved because he asked him the third time, “Do you have affection for me?” He said to him, “Lord, you know everything. You know that I have affection for you.” Jesus said to him, “Feed my sheep.

Kikuyu: “Ngũkwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, rĩrĩa warĩ mwĩthĩ nĩwehumbaga nguo, ũgathiĩ kũrĩa ũkwenda; no rĩrĩa ũgaakũra, nĩũgatambũrũkia moko, nake mũndũ ũngĩ nĩagakũhumba nguo, acooke agũtware kũrĩa ũtekwenda gũthiĩ.”
English: Most certainly I tell you, when you were young, you dressed yourself and walked where you wanted to. But when you are old, you will stretch out your hands, and another will dress you and carry you where you don’t want to go.”

Kikuyu: Jesũ oigire ũguo nĩgeetha onanie gĩkuũ kĩrĩa Petero agaakua, agoocithie Ngai. Agĩcooka akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Nũmĩrĩra!”
English: Now he said this, signifying by what kind of death he would glorify God. When he had said this, he said to him, “Follow me.”

Kikuyu: Nake Petero eehũgũra akĩona mũrutwo ũrĩa Jesũ endete amarũmĩrĩire. (Ũcio nĩ ũrĩa wetiranĩtie na Jesũ rĩrĩa marĩĩaga irio cia hwaĩ-inĩ, na nowe wamũũrĩtie atĩrĩ, “Mwathani, nũũ ũcio ũgũgũkun- yanĩra?”)
English: Then Peter, turning around, saw a disciple following. This was the disciple whom Jesus loved, the one who had also leaned on Jesus’ chest at the supper and asked, “Lord, who is going to betray you?”

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa Petero aamuonire-rĩ, akĩũria atĩrĩ, “Mwathani, nake mũndũ ũyũ ũhoro wake nĩ atĩa?”
English: Peter, seeing him, said to Jesus, “Lord, what about this man?”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Ingĩenda atũũre muoyo nginya rĩrĩa ngacooka-rĩ, ũcio ũkĩrĩ ũhoro waku? Wee no nginya ũnũmĩrĩre.”
English: Jesus said to him, “If I desire that he stay until I come, what is that to you? You follow me.”

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ wa ũguo-rĩ, mũhuhu ũkĩhunja thĩinĩ wa ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ gũkĩĩrwo atĩ mũrutwo ũcio ndagakua. No Jesũ ndoigĩte atĩ ndagakua; ũrĩa oigĩte no atĩ, “Ingĩenda atũũre muoyo nginya rĩrĩa ngacooka-rĩ, ũcio ũkĩrĩ ũhoro waku?”
English: This saying therefore went out among the brothers§ that this disciple wouldn’t die. Yet Jesus didn’t say to him that he wouldn’t die, but, “If I desire that he stay until I come, what is that to you?”

Kikuyu: Mũrutwo ũcio nĩwe ũrĩ na ũira wa maũndũ maya mothe, na nowe ũmaandĩkĩte. Na ithuĩ nĩtũũĩ atĩ ũira wake nĩ wa ma.
English: This is the disciple who testifies about these things, and wrote these things. We know that his witness is true.

Kikuyu: Jesũ nĩekire maũndũ mangĩ maingĩ. Korwo mothe nĩmandĩkirwo-rĩ, ngwĩgereria atĩ o na thĩ yothe ndĩngĩiganĩra mabuku marĩa mangĩandĩkwo.
English: There are also many other things which Jesus did, which if they would all be written, I suppose that even the world itself wouldn’t have room for the books that would be written.
